ddls:
CREATE TABLE "service_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service request */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the specific service requested */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category to which the service belongs */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department associated with the request */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the request was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the request was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the request (e.g., open, in progress, closed) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the request (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the request is assigned */,
  "requestor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the request */,
  "expected_resolution_date" TEXT /* Expected date for resolution of the request */,
  "actual_resolution_date" TEXT /* Actual date when the request was resolved */,
  "sla_breached" INTEGER /* Indicates if the SLA was breached */,
  "resolution_summary" TEXT /* Summary of how the request was resolved */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_requestor_id FOREIGN KEY ("requestor_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category to which the service belongs */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department responsible for the service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "service_code" TEXT /* Unique code or identifier for the service */,
  "sla_hours" INTEGER /* Service Level Agreement (SLA) in hours */,
  "service_cost" TEXT /* Cost associated with the service */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the service is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department responsible for the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the category is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., employee, manager, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for notifications */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being logged */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken (e.g., created, updated, closed) */,
  "action_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was taken */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or additional information about the action */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to resolve the request in minutes */,
  "is_automated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the action was automated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_logs_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_logs_action_by FOREIGN KEY ("action_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being rated */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the requestor (1-5 scale) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or additional feedback provided by the requestor */,
  "submitted_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the feedback was submitted */,
  "is_anonymous" INTEGER /* Indicates if the feedback is anonymous */,
  "satisfaction_score" INTEGER /* Detailed satisfaction score (1-10 scale) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_feedback_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)
